intro: Eremospatha macrocarpa, commonly known as the West African Raphia palm, is a type of plant native to tropical Africa, characterized by its tall, slender stem and large, fan-shaped leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its potential health benefits in reducing inflammation and improving immune function. The bioactive constituents of Eremospatha macrocarpa include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
